title: Client Feedback Evidence CLI
description: Client Feedback Evidence collects chats, voice notes, and emails into one local workflow for usable proof.
seoTitle: Client Feedback Evidence CLI Case Study | Rust Workflow Automation
seoDescription: A case study on a Rust CLI for collecting client feedback evidence from WhatsApp Web and Gmail into local JSON, Markdown, attachment, audio, and transcript artifacts.
constraints: Client feedback can include private message text, audio, and attachments, so the tool had to keep run outputs local by default.
WhatsApp evidence had to come from a linked browser session because the project does not use an official WhatsApp API.
Gmail evidence had to preserve reusable artifacts without exposing OAuth tokens, browser storage, or mailbox runtime state.
problem: Useful client proof was spread across WhatsApp chats, voice notes, and domain Gmail threads, which made it hard to assemble a reusable evidence packet without manual copying and fragile exports.
The workflow needed repeatable collection, local transcription, attachment capture, and manifests without sending sensitive evidence to another ingestion service.
solution: Built a Rust CLI with `doctor`, WhatsApp `probe/search/pull`, and Gmail `search/pull` commands, all available with stable JSON output for automation.

Used a dedicated Agent Browser profile for WhatsApp Web, extracted visible chat messages, downloaded visible voice notes through the message context menu, normalized Agent Browser download artifacts, converted Ogg/Opus audio to WAV, and optionally transcribed locally with `whisper-cpp`.
Added a Gmail rail through `gws` that resolves search results, messages, or threads, decodes attachment payloads locally, writes evidence Markdown, and records hashes for materialized files.
result: Turned scattered client feedback sources into a repeatable local workflow that writes `run.json`, `messages.json`, `evidence.md`, attachments, audio files, WAV files, and transcript artifacts.
Kept the public code focused on workflow contracts and local artifacts while leaving private evidence directories, auth state, cookies, and browser storage out of the repo.

Backed the brittle parts with unit tests for WhatsApp metadata parsing, Agent Browser snapshot parsing, download normalization, Gmail attachment decoding, filename sanitization, and command-output JSON parsing.
